title: Dress Sense
author: Priya
content:
Clothes r our need, necessity, a choice, and a feeling of being looked good. We wear what appeals to us. We designed our wearables as per our body type, how sleek, sedan, or fleshy we r. Clothes can be gender specific or gender neutral, but serve the only purpose to keep us bodily active and socially present. They act like a windshield, a protective layer to protect us from external agents of sun, air, dust, water, and winter.


The type of clothes we wear speaks a lot for us in terms of our cultural values, ethnicity, our social status or class, but tells nothing about our behaviour, mental adaptability, or our social status in a society. What we wear and how we dress differentiate us from others. A good fashion sense gives a symbolic representation of our identity and also of our character as an individual. If we dress properly, it builds our confidence and makes us into believing as someone who has broken all charm of social enigma by representing ourself distinctive and more self-assured with our clean-cut, salient look.  


Current trend and vogue culture -

Can u think what drives us crazy to buy more when u have all set of clothings to satisfy your need, it's our fashion sense and the era of modernization, where we want to look more classy, immaculate, and high profile. We choose luxury over necessity, go for a swanky, flashy look rather than our own comfort. Wearing in-breathable clothes tight to your skin, clothes that abashedly expose your body, or clothes which r much discomforting to the body r becoming a flex these days when your outer appearance and personality exceed all your bodily pleasure and comfort. A desire to look way better than others and to be a discriminating icon among others by dressing as 1 of a kind, nowhere to be found, r drifting us towards adopting a fashion sense that is much more prevalent and reputable in public. And we're blowing money like water for such glitz and glamour, name and fame, and a sterling image in society.


Some dark sides of our offbeat, admirable dress sense - 

Our unconventional style of dressing and weird outlook towards what to wear creates a wrong picture in the minds of youth in making them to follow their fashion divas' obdurate fashion sense. They tend to robe in same style of clothings considering it as 1 of a delectable, star-like, personable look, but in actuality, they're dragging themselves more towards profanity and absurdity with their bold outfit. Our sheer, over-revealing clothing greatly impacts kids mentally, and they create a very negative perception of us as of some outlandish, vulgarian person.

Our culture, our ethnicity slowly becoming extinct because of our crepe, crop clothing style, and is ripping our cultural values with the adoption of western, inconvenient, foppish outfits. Not just that, making oneself look more unslovenly rich, attractive harms our self-respect in society when we become a close victim of crimes like rape, molestation, and sexual abuse. When someone passes lewd comments on us or harasses us publicly for sexual favour, there is no law or authority that can stand by us, as it's we who are to blame for such misconduct because of our bizarre fashion sense. And we don't even realise that to look fancy, to flaunt ourselves, we're burning a hole in our own pocket, dipping into our own savings, using our stockpile of money into an extravaganza which could be utilised into something much more useful and endowing.

Whether you are black or white, a dress will only give u an external beauty it cannot justify your character. Your inner strength lies in your heart. If u help a person in need, u will get many helping hands in return. With your fancy, dazzling outfit u may get many eyes gazing at u but all helping hands to those who have a heart of gold and are always ready to ride or die for you. 

Every profession has a distinctive identity that is differentiated with their uniform. So, a dress plays a very important role in giving you that unique identity. A doctor, a lawyer, or an army officer are all our front warriors with a uniform that uniquely identifies them, and we honour their prestigious roles and service to the nation. And bestow them and their uniform with the great love and respect that we have for our national flag.

A cloth with a mumsy look or an overly flashy outfit gives u an external presence of looking good or normal, and is just our state of mind. A cloth can define a heroic personality, but can't necessarily say whether u r that heroic or hollow from inside. No matter how plain or simple your outfit is in the end, it is seen how committed u r on your moral grounds. Your strength and your genuinity lies within u which is much more dearer than your expensive, cost-worthy outfit u wear.
